text: Good evening. You made it through another day — and that means something. This isn't a performance review. It's a mirror. Let's take ten minutes to look at how you actually showed up today.
If today were a weather report, what would it be?
options: Sunny — things flowed
Overcast — sluggish but manageable
Stormy — things fell apart
Unpredictable — it kept changing
answer=Sunny — things flowed|Unpredictable — it kept changing:A1_Q_AGENCY_HIGH
answer=Overcast — sluggish but manageable|Stormy — things fell apart:A1_Q_AGENCY_LOW

text: You described today as '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When something went well, what would you say made it happen?
options: I prepared and it paid off
The team delivered
I caught a lucky break
I noticed a problem and adapted

text: You described today as '{A1_OPEN.answer}'. When things got difficult, what was your first instinct?
options: Figure out what I could still control
Wait for someone to step in
Push through it on my own
Feel stuck and unsure what to do next
answer=I prepared and it paid off|I noticed a problem and adapted:A1_Q2_INTERNAL
answer=The team delivered|I caught a lucky break:A1_Q2_CREDIT
answer=Figure out what I could still control|Push through it on my own:A1_Q2_INTERNAL
answer=Wait for someone to step in|Feel stuck and unsure what to do next:A1_Q2_EXTERNAL

text: Think of one specific moment today where things were not going your way. Did you make a choice in that moment — even a small one?
options: Yes — and I can name what that choice was
Sort of — I reacted, but it wasn't deliberate
I'm not sure I had a choice
I did nothing and let it pass

text: When things went right today, did you feel like you could claim any credit — or did it feel like it happened around you?
options: I contributed — I can say specifically how
I was part of it, but the team carried it
I got lucky more than I deserved
I'm honestly not sure what I did

text: When the day was difficult, who or what came to mind first?
options: A person who created the problem
A situation I couldn't control
Something I probably should have handled earlier
I'm not sure — it just felt heavy
answer=Yes — and I can name what that choice was|Sort of — I reacted, but it wasn't deliberate:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=I'm not sure I had a choice|I did nothing and let it pass:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=I contributed — I can say specifically how|I was part of it, but the team carried it:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=I got lucky more than I deserved|I'm honestly not sure what I did:A1_R_EXTERNAL
answer=Something I probably should have handled earlier:A1_R_INTERNAL
answer=A person who created the problem|A situation I couldn't control|I'm not sure — it just felt heavy:A1_R_EXTERNAL

text: You see your own hand in today. That's not self-blame — that's agency. People who notice where they had a choice, even a small one, are the ones who can grow from what happened. Whatever today threw at you, some part of you was in the driver's seat.

A hard day pulls attention outward — to what others did or didn't do, to circumstances that weren't in your favor. That's a real response to real difficulty. But there's usually a moment, even a small one, where you had a choice. You don't have to claim the whole day — just one moment.
You've looked at how you handled today. Now let's look at what you gave.
You've looked at how you handled today. Now let's turn the lens — not on what happened to you, but on what you brought to others.
Think about one specific interaction you had today — a meeting, a conversation, a message. Which of these feels closest to how it went?
options: I gave something — time, energy, help — without tracking the return
I was present, but mostly doing what was expected of me
I noticed I was keeping score a bit
I felt like I gave more than I received today
answer=I gave something — time, energy, help — without tracking the return|I was present, but mostly doing what was expected of me:A2_Q_CONTRIBUTION
answer=I noticed I was keeping score a bit|I felt like I gave more than I received today:A2_Q_ENTITLEMENT

text: Was there a moment today where you did something for someone that wasn't asked of you, wasn't tracked, and had no direct benefit to you?
options: Yes — I can picture it clearly
Maybe — something small, not sure it counts
No, I was heads-down on my own work
I thought about it but didn't act on it

text: When you thought about recognition or credit today — what came up?
options: I felt my work was seen and that felt good
I did good work but nobody acknowledged it
I didn't think much about recognition at all
I felt frustrated that someone else got credit I deserved
answer=Yes — I can picture it clearly|Maybe — something small, not sure it counts:A2_Q2_GIVING
answer=No, I was heads-down on my own work|I thought about it but didn't act on it:A2_Q2_WITHHOLD
answer=I felt my work was seen and that felt good|I didn't think much about recognition at all:A2_Q2_GIVING
answer=I did good work but nobody acknowledged it|I felt frustrated that someone else got credit I deserved:A2_Q2_WITHHOLD

text: When you think about your team's progress today — not just your own tasks — how much mental space did that take up?
options: A lot — I was thinking about us as a unit
Some — I checked in on a couple of people
Not much — I was in execution mode
Barely any — I had my own fires to manage

text: Is there something you held back today — effort, knowledge, or support — that someone else could have used?
options: Honestly, yes — I could have offered more
I was stretched thin, there was nothing left to give
I didn't notice anyone needed anything
I thought about helping but wasn't sure it was my place
answer=A lot — I was thinking about us as a unit|Some — I checked in on a couple of people:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Not much — I was in execution mode|Barely any — I had my own fires to manage:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION
answer=Honestly, yes — I could have offered more|I thought about helping but wasn't sure it was my place:A2_R_ENTITLEMENT
answer=I was stretched thin, there was nothing left to give|I didn't notice anyone needed anything:A2_R_CONTRIBUTION

text: Contribution isn't about being selfless to the point of burning out. It's about the direction of your attention — are you oriented toward what you can give, or toward what you're owed? Today, at least some of your energy was pointed outward. That's worth noticing.

Keeping score is human — especially on hard days. But the question is: who are you keeping score with? If the score is you vs. recognition, you'll always feel behind. If the score is you vs. yesterday's effort, you always have a chance. What would it look like to give one thing tomorrow that you don't expect anything back for?
You've looked at how you handled the day, and what you gave. Now for the final lens — how wide did your concern reach today?
You've looked at how you handled the day, and how you oriented yourself. Now the final question — who else were you thinking about today?
When you think about today's biggest challenge, whose face — if anyone's — comes to mind beyond your own?
options: No one specific — it felt like my problem to solve
A teammate who was in it with me
Someone who needed something from me that I may not have fully delivered
The people on the other end of my work — users, clients, those I serve
answer=No one specific — it felt like my problem to solve:A3_Q_SELF
answer=A teammate who was in it with me|Someone who needed something from me that I may not have fully delivered|The people on the other end of my work — users, clients, those I serve:A3_Q_OTHER

text: At any point today, did you wonder how someone else was experiencing the same situation — not to fix it, just to understand?
options: Yes — I actually took a moment to imagine it
A little — it crossed my mind briefly
I was too absorbed in my own tasks
No — I wasn't in that kind of headspace today

text: When you thought about that person — the teammate, the colleague, the person you serve — what did you do with that awareness?
options: I acted on it — I reached out or adjusted something
I held it in mind, even if I couldn't act
I noticed it, then moved on to my own priorities
I felt it, but I didn't know what to do with it
answer=Yes — I actually took a moment to imagine it|A little — it crossed my mind briefly:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=I was too absorbed in my own tasks|No — I wasn't in that kind of headspace today:A3_Q2_NARROW
answer=I acted on it — I reached out or adjusted something|I held it in mind, even if I couldn't act:A3_Q2_EXPAND
answer=I noticed it, then moved on to my own priorities|I felt it, but I didn't know what to do with it:A3_Q2_NARROW

text: Is there someone on your team who had a harder day than you today — and did you notice?
options: Yes — I checked in on them
Yes — I noticed but didn't act on it
I'm not sure — I wasn't tuned in that way
No, everyone seemed fine from where I was sitting

text: When today felt heavy, what was the frame you were using to experience it?
options: My workload, my stress, my goals
My team's performance and what it meant for us
What this meant for the people we serve
I wasn't framing it — I was just surviving it
answer=Yes — I checked in on them|Yes — I noticed but didn't act on it:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=I'm not sure — I wasn't tuned in that way|No, everyone seemed fine from where I was sitting:A3_R_SELF
answer=My team's performance and what it meant for us|What this meant for the people we serve:A3_R_ALTROCENTRIC
answer=My workload, my stress, my goals|I wasn't framing it — I was just surviving it:A3_R_SELF

text: Your concern reaches beyond yourself — and that's not a small thing. Research consistently shows that people who orient toward others' wellbeing don't just help more, they also suffer less. Meaning isn't found by looking inward. It's found by looking outward and acting from there.

Today your radius of concern was narrow — mostly just yourself. That's not a judgment; sometimes the day demands exactly that. But there's a question worth sitting with: is there one person who might have needed something from you today, that you didn't notice? You don't have to fix it now. Just hold the question.

Here's what today looked like, honestly:

On agency, you leaned {axis1.dominant} — {axis1.summary}.

On contribution, you showed up {axis2.dominant} — {axis2.summary}.

On radius, your concern reached {axis3.dominant} — {axis3.summary}.

Three numbers don't define you. But three honest answers might tell you something worth knowing. Sleep on it.

axis1.internal: you saw where you had a hand in things
axis1.external: you felt pulled by what was outside your control

axis2.contribution: oriented toward what you could give
axis2.entitlement: more aware of what you felt was owed to you

axis3.altrocentric: beyond yourself — to teammates and the people you serve
axis3.self: close to home — your own tasks, your own load

text: That's it. You showed up, you reflected, and that matters. See you tomorrow.
